text.title: MCP Core Rules
text.summary: Seven testable rules for shipping an MCP server that LLM clients can actually discover, call, and operate against.

rule.statement: Every MCP server MUST expose a tool named `ping` (or `health`) returning `{"status":"ok","version":"<server.version>"}` so clients can verify availability before invoking domain tools.
rule.rationale: Without a probe, agents cannot distinguish "server down" from "tool returned empty" — both surface as a generic connection error on the next call.

rule.statement: Tool names MUST be globally unique across all servers registered in the client (verify with `mcp-inspector list-tools --all`). Use a server-prefix scheme (`weather.get_current`, `db.run_query`) when collisions are likely.
rule.rationale: Two servers exposing `search` causes non-deterministic dispatch — the client picks one and ignores the other without raising.

rule.statement: Tool `inputSchema` MUST be flat (no `oneOf`, `anyOf`, `$ref`, or nested polymorphism deeper than 2 levels). Use separate tools instead of polymorphic dispatch.
rule.rationale: LLMs interpret polymorphic JSON Schema poorly; a flat schema with explicit `required` fields is what tool selection actually trains on.

rule.statement: Pick transport by client topology: stdio when server runs co-located with the client (single process), Streamable HTTP when ≥2 clients or remote. SSE-only transport is deprecated post-2025-03-26 spec — do not author new servers on it.
rule.rationale: Streamable HTTP supersedes SSE because it works on commodity HTTP infra; stdio is still optimal for local single-process scenarios.

rule.statement: Remote Streamable HTTP servers MUST act as OAuth 2.1 Resource Servers — advertise `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource` and validate bearer tokens against the declared issuer. Servers without auth MUST NOT bind on `0.0.0.0`.
rule.rationale: Public remote MCP without auth is the canonical exfiltration vector since June 2025 spec; OAuth Resource Server is the standard posture.

rule.statement: Embed `server_version` in every tool response payload (string, semver). Bump on every breaking change; keep N-1 parser registered for one rollback window.
rule.rationale: Without per-payload versioning, downstream agents silently corrupt state when a tool's response shape drifts.

rule.statement: Tool errors MUST be returned as `{"error":{"code":"<machine_code>","message":"<human>","retryable":<bool>}}` in the result text — never raise to the client transport layer. Reserve `retryable=true` for timeouts and 5xx-class upstream failures.
rule.rationale: Base MCP spec only carries string errors at transport; a structured envelope inside the result lets agents branch on `retryable` without parsing prose.
